sourceMovie.title: Red Eye
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Red Eye works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Salt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Red Eye clicked for you, Salt (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Red Eye?
faq.answer: Try Salt, Non-Stop, Bordertown—each shares DNA with Red Eye while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Red Eye?
faq.answer: Red Eye (2005) is best described as Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Red Eye worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
